question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger integer overflow in `backoff(base, attempt)` by causing `cfg.maxRetries` to be set to a value >= 64 via the `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES` environment variable, so that `1<<(attempt-1)` overflows `time.Duration` (int64), producing a negative or near-zero sleep duration that causes the retry loop in `submitWithRetry()` to spin at maximum speed, flooding the consensus nodes with malformed transactions and causing total network transaction confirmation failure?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence `cfg.maxRetries` (via `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES`) set it to `math.MaxInt - 1` so that `attempts := cfg.maxRetries + 1` overflows to a negative number, causing the loop `for i := 1; i <= attempts` to never execute, silently dropping all transaction submissions and causing the network to appear unable to confirm new transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR` to `0` (bypassing the `cfg.amountTinybar == 0` check by injecting whitespace or a non-zero value that resolves to zero after parsing) so that `AddHbarTransfer` submits a zero-value transfer, causing consensus nodes to reject all transactions with `INVALID_TRANSACTION_BODY` and permanently preventing transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR` to a large negative value so that `AddHbarTransfer(client.GetOperatorAccountID(), hiero.HbarFromTinybar(-cfg.amountTinybar))` produces a positive debit larger than the operator's balance, causing every transaction to fail with `INSUFFICIENT_ACCOUNT_BALANCE` and exhausting all retries, resulting in zero confirmed transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID` to a syntactically valid but non-existent account ID string so that `hiero.AccountIDFromString(cfg.toAccountID)` succeeds but every `Execute(client)` call returns `INVALID_ACCOUNT_ID`, causing all retry attempts to fail and permanently halting transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID` to a malformed string (e.g., `0.0.`) that passes `AccountIDFromString` parsing in some SDK versions but causes the constructed transaction to be rejected by all consensus nodes, making `Execute(client)` always return an error and preventing any transaction from ever being confirmed?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF` to an extremely large duration (e.g., `8760h`) so that `backoff(cfg.baseBackoff, 1)` returns 30 seconds (capped) but intermediate attempts use the uncapped value, causing the retry loop to block for hours per tick, effectively halting all transaction submissions and causing total network confirmation failure from the pinger's perspective?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF` to a value such that `base * time.Duration(1<<(attempt-1))` overflows `int64` before the `min(..., 30*time.Second)` cap is applied, producing a negative duration that causes `time.NewTimer` to fire immediately, removing all backoff delay and causing the retry loop to hammer consensus nodes at maximum rate, triggering rate-limiting or node bans that prevent legitimate transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL` to `1ns` (minimum valid duration) so that the ticker in `main.go` fires at nanosecond intervals, causing `submitWithRetry()` to be called in a tight loop, flooding the Hiero SDK client with concurrent `Execute(client)` calls, exhausting connection pools, and causing all transaction submissions to fail with connection errors, resulting in total network confirmation failure?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL` to `0` or a negative value, and since `toDuration()` falls back to `1*time.Second` on parse error but `cfg.interval <= 0` check only catches zero/negative after flag parsing, cause a misconfiguration that results in the ticker never firing or firing at an unintended rate, halting all transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user who can set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY` to a syntactically valid but cryptographically wrong private key cause `newClient()` to succeed (key parses fine) but every `Execute(client)` call to fail with `INVALID_SIGNATURE`, exhausting all `cfg.maxRetries` attempts per tick and causing permanent transaction confirmation failure across the entire pinger lifetime?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_ID` to a valid-format but wrong account ID (e.g., an account that does not match the operator key) so that `client.SetOperator(opID, opKey)` succeeds but every signed transaction is rejected by consensus nodes with `INVALID_SIGNATURE` or `PAYER_ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND`, causing all retries to fail and halting transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a goroutine leak by setting `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES` to a very large value, so that each ticker tick spawns a long-running `submitWithRetry()` call that blocks for the full retry duration, and since `main.go` calls `submitWithRetry()` synchronously in the ticker loop, subsequent ticks are skipped, causing the pinger to miss transaction submission windows and the network to appear unable to confirm transactions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `resp.GetReceipt(client)` in `submitWithRetry()` has no explicit timeout, so that if a consensus node hangs after accepting a transaction, `GetReceipt` blocks indefinitely, causing the entire ticker loop in `main.go` to stall and preventing any subsequent transactions from being submitted, resulting in total network confirmation failure?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `cryptoTransfer.Execute(client)` in `submitWithRetry()` has no explicit per-call timeout (only the parent context), so that a slow or unresponsive consensus node causes `Execute` to block indefinitely, stalling the ticker loop and preventing all future transaction submissions?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK` to `other` and `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST` to a URL pointing to an attacker-controlled server that returns a valid JSON `nodesEnvelope` with all nodes pointing to non-existent or attacker-controlled gRPC endpoints, causing `buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()` to succeed but every `Execute(client)` call in `submitWithRetry()` to fail with connection errors, halting all transaction confirmations?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK` to `other` and point `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST` to an attacker-controlled server that returns nodes with `service_endpoints` pointing to `127.0.0.1` on closed ports, causing `hiero.ClientForNetwork(netmap)` to build a client that always fails `Execute(client)` with connection refused, permanently preventing transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK` to `other` and serve a mirror REST response with `node_account_id` values that parse successfully but map to wrong consensus node accounts, causing `AddHbarTransfer` to build valid-looking transactions that are routed to wrong nodes and rejected, exhausting all retries and halting confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR` to `math.MinInt64` so that `-cfg.amountTinybar` overflows to `math.MinInt64` again (since `-math.MinInt64 == math.MinInt64` in two's complement), causing `hiero.HbarFromTinybar` to receive an unexpected value and the resulting transaction to be rejected by all consensus nodes, halting confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR` to `math.MaxInt64` so that `AddHbarTransfer(toID, hiero.HbarFromTinybar(cfg.amountTinybar))` attempts to credit an astronomically large amount, causing every transaction to fail with `INSUFFICIENT_ACCOUNT_BALANCE` or `INVALID_TRANSACTION_BODY`, exhausting all retries and preventing any transaction from being confirmed?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external user set `HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES` to exactly 63 so that on the last attempt `backoff(base, 63)` computes `base * (1<<62)` which overflows `time.Duration` (int64), producing a negative timer duration, causing `time.NewTimer` to panic or fire immediately, disrupting the retry loop and causing unpredictable transaction submission behavior that prevents confirmation?
